From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [1590s in Belgium](/w/index.php?title=1590s_in_Belgium&redirect=no "1590s in Belgium"))

|  |
| --- |
| **1590s in Belgium:** |
| *Other decades* |
| [1570s](/w/index.php?title=1570s_in_Belgium&action=edit&redlink=1 "1570s in Belgium (page does not exist)") | [1580s](/wiki/1580s_in_Belgium "1580s in Belgium") | **1590s** | [1600s](/w/index.php?title=1600s_in_Belgium&action=edit&redlink=1 "1600s in Belgium (page does not exist)") | [1610s](/w/index.php?title=1610s_in_Belgium&action=edit&redlink=1 "1610s in Belgium (page does not exist)") |

Events from the **1590s in the [Spanish Netherlands](/wiki/Spanish_Netherlands "Spanish Netherlands") and [Prince-bishopric of Liège](/wiki/Prince-bishopric_of_Li%C3%A8ge "Prince-bishopric of Liège")**.

## Incumbents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1590s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Incumbents")]

### Habsburg Netherlands

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1590s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Habsburg Netherlands")]

Monarch

* [Philip II](/wiki/Philip_II_of_Spain "Philip II of Spain"), King of Spain and [Duke of Brabant](/wiki/Dukes_of_Brabant "Dukes of Brabant"), [of Luxembourg](/wiki/Counts,_Dukes_and_Grand_Dukes_of_Luxembourg "Counts, Dukes and Grand Dukes of Luxembourg"), etc. to September 1598
* [Isabella Clara Eugenia](/wiki/Isabella_Clara_Eugenia "Isabella Clara Eugenia") and [Albert VII, Archduke of Austria](/wiki/Albert_VII,_Archduke_of_Austria "Albert VII, Archduke of Austria"), as co-sovereigns from September 1598.

Governor General

* [Alexander Farnese](/wiki/Alexander_Farnese,_Duke_of_Parma "Alexander Farnese, Duke of Parma"), Duke of Parma, to 1592
* [Peter Ernst I von Mansfeld-Vorderort](/wiki/Peter_Ernst_I_von_Mansfeld-Vorderort "Peter Ernst I von Mansfeld-Vorderort") 1592–1594
* [Archduke Ernest of Austria](/wiki/Archduke_Ernest_of_Austria "Archduke Ernest of Austria") 1594–1595
* [Pedro Henriquez de Acevedo, Count of Fuentes](/wiki/Pedro_Henriquez_de_Acevedo,_Count_of_Fuentes "Pedro Henriquez de Acevedo, Count of Fuentes"), acting governor general 1595–1596
* [Albert VII, Archduke of Austria](/wiki/Albert_VII,_Archduke_of_Austria "Albert VII, Archduke of Austria") 1596–1598
* [Margrave Andrew of Burgau](/wiki/Margrave_Andrew_of_Burgau "Margrave Andrew of Burgau"), acting governor general 1598–1599

### Prince-Bishopric of Liège

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1590s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Prince-Bishopric of Liège")]

Prince-Bishop

* [Ernest of Bavaria](/wiki/Ernest_of_Bavaria "Ernest of Bavaria")

## Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1590s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Events")]

1590

* 4 March – [Loss of Breda](/wiki/Capture_of_Breda_(1590) "Capture of Breda (1590)")

1591

1592

* 28 May – [Diocesan Seminary of Liège](/wiki/Diocesan_Seminary_of_Li%C3%A8ge "Diocesan Seminary of Liège") opens

1593

1594

* [Joyous Entry](/wiki/Joyous_Entry "Joyous Entry") into Brussels and Antwerp of [Archduke Ernest of Austria](/wiki/Archduke_Ernest_of_Austria "Archduke Ernest of Austria")

1595

* 7 to 20 March – [Siege of Huy](/wiki/Siege_of_Huy_(1595) "Siege of Huy (1595)")
* 14 October – Loss and retaking of Lier
* 28 December – [Ernest of Bavaria](/wiki/Ernest_of_Bavaria "Ernest of Bavaria") appoints André Streignart, [Jean Chapeauville](/wiki/Jean_Chapeauville "Jean Chapeauville"), Pierre Oranus, and Jean Malempeter to an [inquisitorial](/wiki/Inquisition "Inquisition") [witchcraft tribunal](/wiki/Witch_trials_in_the_early_modern_period "Witch trials in the early modern period") to investigate and judge the case of [Jean Delvaux](/wiki/Jean_Delvaux "Jean Delvaux"), a monk of [Stavelot Abbey](/wiki/Stavelot_Abbey "Stavelot Abbey") who had voluntarily confessed to attending [gatherings of witches](/wiki/Witches%27_Sabbath "Witches' Sabbath").[[1]](#cite_note-Chapeauville-1)

1596

* 24 April – Taking of Calais
* 23 May – Taking of Ardres

1597

* 24 January – [Battle of Turnhout](/wiki/Battle_of_Turnhout_(1597) "Battle of Turnhout (1597)")
* 2 April – Condemnation of [Jean Delvaux](/wiki/Jean_Delvaux "Jean Delvaux") for witchcraft[[1]](#cite_note-Chapeauville-1)

1598

* 2 May – [Peace of Vervins](/wiki/Peace_of_Vervins "Peace of Vervins") concluded

1599

* 18 April – Marriage of [Isabella Clara Eugenia](/wiki/Isabella_Clara_Eugenia "Isabella Clara Eugenia") and [Albert VII, Archduke of Austria](/wiki/Albert_VII,_Archduke_of_Austria "Albert VII, Archduke of Austria")
* 5 September – [Joyous Entry](/wiki/Joyous_Entry "Joyous Entry") into Brussels of [Isabella Clara Eugenia](/wiki/Isabella_Clara_Eugenia "Isabella Clara Eugenia") and [Albert VII, Archduke of Austria](/wiki/Albert_VII,_Archduke_of_Austria "Albert VII, Archduke of Austria"), as co-sovereigns
